# Excavator Foot — Factory-Installable Pivot Technology for Tracked Excavators

> The Excavator Foot is a patented hydraulic plate-and-turntable mechanism that lets a tracked excavator rotate in place without skid-turning the tracks. It mounts to the underside of the main frame and uses the excavator's own existing rotary motor — anchored via the boom and bucket — to perform the rotation. U.S. Patent Application 18/594,097 has received a non-final office action with allowable subject matter indicated for the apparatus and the excavator-combination claims. The technology is offered for OEM licensing to manufacturers of tracked construction equipment.

## Positioning

This is **OEM licensing technology**, not a retail aftermarket product. The Excavator Foot is designed to be a factory-installable option on production tracked excavators in the 2-ton through 12-ton class range. Three working prototypes (on Caterpillar 302, 305, and 308 chassis) have been built and operated to validate the mechanism across machine classes.

## The Problem It Solves

Every tracked excavator on the market has the same physical limitation: to change orientation, the tracks must skid-turn against the ground. This single design flaw produces ongoing operating costs that the OEM does not absorb:

- **Surface damage** — lawns, asphalt, concrete, pavers, and finished hardscape are gouged on every rotation, with restoration cost borne by the contractor
- **Undercarriage wear** — skid-turning is the dominant driver of premature track, sprocket, and roller replacement, often the largest non-fuel operating cost on the machine
- **Restricted addressable jobsites** — finished surfaces, decorative hardscape, and sensitive turf force operators to substitute wheeled equipment, shrinking the market for tracked machines
- **Liability exposure** — property damage from track-tear is a routine source of disputes in residential, right-of-way, and urban infill work

There is no existing factory or aftermarket solution that addresses this at the machine-design level using the host machine's own systems.

## The Invention

The Excavator Foot is a hydraulic plate-and-turntable assembly that mounts to the underside of the excavator main frame. It is operated by the existing in-cab control system and integrates with the host machine's hydraulic circuit. The operational sequence is four steps:

1. **Anchor** — the boom is lowered until the bucket engages the ground. The cabin is now mechanically fixed relative to the earth via the boom-bucket-ground chain.
2. **Lift** — the hydraulic linkage drives the turntable assembly downward. As it contacts the ground, the tracks lift clear and the entire main frame is supported on the foot.
3. **Rotate** — with the cabin anchored by the bucket, the excavator's own rotary motor spins the main frame and tracks freely on the turntable to any heading.
4. **Retract** — the foot retracts, the tracks settle on the ground at the new heading, and the bucket releases. Net result: a full reorientation of the machine with zero ground scrub and zero track wear.

## Why This Is Hard to Design Around

The mechanism uses the excavator's existing rotary motor — not a new powertrain. This is what makes it economically viable as a factory option. But it also creates a physics-based moat:

Without the bucket-anchor step, firing the rotary motor with the tracks lifted would simply counter-rotate the cabin against the tracks (Newton's third law) — net rotation: zero. The bucket-anchor step is not a design choice. It is a physical requirement of any system that uses the host machine's own rotary motor to perform the rotation.

Any competing implementation that uses a different rotation mechanism (a dedicated turntable motor, for example) adds powertrain cost, hydraulic complexity, and parts count — and still infringes the combination claim. The path of least resistance for an OEM is to license, not design around.

## Engineering Validation

Three working prototypes have been built and operated:

- **2-ton class** — Caterpillar 302 — mechanism geometry validated, operator sequence proven
- **5-ton class** — Caterpillar 305 — full operational cycle on dirt and pavement, featured in the public demonstration video at https://excavatorfoot.com/#demo
- **8-ton class** — Caterpillar 308 — production-grade machined parts in final assembly; live demonstration available Q3 2026

The 308 prototype is the size threshold where right-of-way contractors, urban infill builders, and infrastructure crews operate. It is the prototype that demonstrates the mechanism scales to the machine classes OEMs ship in volume.

## Intellectual Property

- **U.S. Patent Application Number**: 18/594,097
- **Filed**: March 4, 2024 (with priority to provisional 63/598,078 filed November 11, 2023)
- **Status**: Non-final office action received April 15, 2026; amendment to take indicated allowable subject matter being prosecuted by Patent Agent Bruce A. Lev (Reg. 58,594)
- **Inventor**: Kable Darren Record
- **Type**: Utility patent — mechanical / hydraulic pivot system for tracked construction equipment

The patent application includes both an apparatus claim (the mechanism itself) and a combination claim (the excavator + apparatus + four-step operational sequence). A continuation application is planned post-issuance to broaden the ground-engagement language to cover equivalent rotation mechanisms beyond the literal turntable form, closing off remaining design-around space.

## Available Materials

**Public:**
- Demonstration video on the Caterpillar 305 prototype
- Operational sequence overview
- Patent application number and prosecution status

**Available under mutual NDA:**
- Engineering drawings, dimensions, and tolerances
- Hydraulic schematics and integration specifications
- Bills of materials and supplier information
- Full claim chart and prosecution history
- Royalty model and territory pricing
- Manufacturing cost analysis
- Detailed 308 demonstration video

A mutual NDA template is available for download at https://excavatorfoot.com/contact (PDF and editable Word formats). Evaluators may also use their own NDA template if preferred.

## Licensing Targets

The technology is targeted for licensing to manufacturers of tracked construction equipment. Primary candidates include:

- Caterpillar Inc. (specifically the Building Construction Products division)
- Bobcat Company (Doosan)
- Kubota Tractor Corporation
- John Deere Construction & Forestry
- Komatsu Ltd.
- Volvo Construction Equipment
- Hitachi Construction Machinery
- JCB
- Takeuchi
- Yanmar

Exclusive territory and field-of-use licensing options are available.

## About the Inventor

Kable Darren Record is a U.S.-based construction entrepreneur and inventor. The Excavator Foot grew out of years of running heavy equipment on residential, right-of-way, and commercial jobsites — and the observation that the most expensive moments on a jobsite are often the ones the equipment cannot avoid.

In addition to this work, Kable is the founder of OSQR (https://osqr.ai), an AI operating system for small businesses, and the author of the Fourth Gen Formula (https://fourthgenformula.com), a framework for generational wealth-building.

His operating preference is to license patented mechanical inventions to OEMs that can integrate them into existing manufacturing programs, rather than build a parallel aftermarket business around them. That is the path being pursued for this technology.

## Contact

- **Website**: https://excavatorfoot.com
- **Email**: contact@excavatorfoot.com
- **Two paths**: (a) schedule a demo (no NDA required) — see the prototype operate and ask engineering questions live; (b) request the technical package (NDA-gated) — drawings, claim chart, royalty model, and manufacturing cost analysis

Initial response within 5 business days for qualified inquiries.
